bird.name: Tasmanian thornbill
bird.summary: The Tasmanian Thornbill (Acanthiza ewingii) is a small bushland member of the Acanthizidae (Australian warbler) family, endemic to Tasmania…

relations.subject: Tasmanian thornbill
relations.evidence: It shares much of its range with the brown thornbill, but tends to occupy the wetter areas of the habitat, often choosing to live in dense scrub around wet gullies rather than the drier, more open slopes. Temperate rainforest has been established to be the preferred habitat of the Tasmanian Thornbill, but its range of suitable habitats also include Mediterranean-style shrubby vegetation, bogs, marshes, fens, swamps, peatlands, and shrub-dominated wetlands. The subspecies A
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Tasmanian thornbill
relations.evidence: The Tasmanian Thornbill is common and widespread throughout Tasmania and the Bass Strait Islands, and has been classified by Birdlife International as a secure species of least concern on the ICUN Red List of Threatened Species. However, due to ongoing habitat destruction and heavy pesticide use, its population is suspected to be in decline
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
